title: Ramirezella buseri

Further classification of Ramirezella buseri includes its order, which is Apiales, and its subclass, which is Rosidae. The classification of Ramirezella buseri helps in understanding its evolutionary relationships and characteristics.

intro: Ramirezella buseri, commonly known as Buseria, is a plant species within the family Apocynaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ramirezella buseri include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
